edit_diagnosis: r_center_guidance dominance prevents descent and landing; no dense vertical progress shaping leads to hovering and zero landings.
Poor landing quality due to weak centering, slow descent, and no speed penalty leads to bouncing and off-center landings. Edits strengthen these aspects while preserving safety.

Terminal landing achieved but unstable contact (bouncing) due to weak near‑ground speed control and no incentive for sustained contact. Package adds a per‑step contact‑stability bonus, a near‑ground velocity penalty, and lateral velocity awareness in vertical safety.

Landing success event triggers too easily, allowing high-speed, unstable touchdowns. The reward lacks near-ground speed penalty and terminal condition alignment. Solution: tighten success condition to require low velocity and angular velocity, add dense speed penalty when y < 0.2, and increase descent weight to maintain progress.

Lander bounces due to missing near-ground velocity penalty, preventing stable contact required for terminal success. Adding an unconditional speed penalty below y=0.2 should encourage softer touchdowns without harming progress.
Current hovering policy caused by descent guidance dead zone (y>1) and excessive center guidance weight (0.5). Fix: early descent reward (3-y) and lower center weight to 0.2.

r_descent_guidance gives constant max reward (3-y clipped to 1 at all altitudes), causing dominance and no descent gradient; agent bounces without landing. Fix: change formula to 2-y, reduce weight, and add contact stability reward.

Shaping‑goal mismatch: r_descent_guidance dominates with constant reward, preventing stable contact. Coupled rebalancing needed – reduce descent weight, boost contact reward, relax vertical safety condition.

r_center_guidance dominates (0.856 ratio) and r_descent_guidance never activates because y>1, causing endless hovering. Coupled edit: reduce center weight to 0.2, replace descent formula to 2.0−y (clipped [0,1]), and increase descent weight to 0.6, providing early descent gradient while keeping safety constraints intact.
